name: TAO Compression Fakeout Reexpansion v1
thesis: TAO 4h is the structurally different lane for testing a compression fakeout into re-expansion with directional confirmation. The edge should come from separating compression as setup from directional confirmation as trigger rather than asking one indicator stack to do every job.
edge_mechanism: compression fakeout followed by directional re-expansion
invalidation_condition: Abort if compression setups are too rare, if fakeout and directional confirmation conflict, or if re-expansion does not survive costs.

pre_submit_reasoning.market_behavior_targeted: Price compresses, fakes one side of balance, then re-expands directionally once the fakeout loses control and acceptance shifts.
pre_submit_reasoning.why_edge_should_exist: Compression often loads energy, but the first move can be false. A structurally different asset like TAO can test whether fakeout-plus-confirmation creates better density than raw breakout chasing.
pre_submit_reasoning.asset_timeframe_fit: TAO 4h is the appropriate structurally different lane because the mechanism needs enough move size to matter and TAO's volatility should stress-test the role separation cleanly.
pre_submit_reasoning.likely_failure_mode: If compression resolves into pure chop instead of directional re-expansion, confirmation may arrive too late or not at all.
pre_submit_reasoning.first_refinement_path: If setups exist but follow-through is weak, simplify the confirmation stack before changing the squeeze threshold.

density_sanity_check.contradictory_filters: roles are separated; compression is setup while EMA/ADX define direction
density_sanity_check.event_sparsity: kept manageable by moderate compression thresholds instead of extreme squeeze-only rules
